s2_high_volume_wa_only.note: Of top-10 DMs, most have no Gmail trail. Natural WA-only seeds.

s3_lid_only_group_sender.note: 111 active LID-only group senders; 99.85% bridge to phone via lid_map — so almost no truly LID-only top seeds. The unbridged tail (2 bare LIDs) are the real test.

s4_gmail_heavy.note: 40 total, ~15-20 after dropping bot-patterns that slipped through; Sanchay's own outbound is the #1 bucket (388)

s5_dormant_then_recent.note: Zero DM chats match (last-30d activity AND prior activity > 365d before). Corpus is too shallow — earliest ts 2022-11-11, but there's no genuine dormant-then-reawakened pattern yet.

probe5_adversarial_grades.note: 1 missing-bridge = wa_contact w/ valid phone but no corresponding wa_dm or Google Contact entry — likely a real human but thin. 0 wrong-merges after @lid-phone-column fix.

cracks_identified.name: unresolvable_nameless_lid_only_contacts
cracks_identified.metric: 4985 nameless @lid contacts have no lid_map entry and no name — 42% of contacts are undifferentiated sludge
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: Deterministic layer cannot produce any card for these. They will always be empty-bucket singletons. Must be filtered out of the seed list, not fed into the resolver.
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Add a seed filter: drop @lid contacts where full_name AND push_name AND first_name are all empty AND lid_map lookup fails. Do NOT try to build cards for these — they are WhatsApp-only group lurkers, not relationships.

cracks_identified.name: contact_phone_column_echoes_lid_digits
cracks_identified.metric: 9948 of 9975 @lid contacts have phone column = LID digits, not a real phone. Without guard, this creates hallucinated E.164 numbers (+88888945160252 etc.) and produces 31 fake wrong-merges in union-find.
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: Poisons cross-source bucketing. Found and guarded in this recon — but any future code that reads contacts.phone without the guard re-introduces the bug.
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Codify guard in observer/orbit-rules: if jid ends @lid, ignore phone column unless the digits differ from the LID. Add a unit test.

cracks_identified.name: gmail_only_buckets_never_join_wa
cracks_identified.metric: 0 overlap between 209 Gmail From canonical emails and 3 Google Contacts emails. Gmail and WhatsApp live on disjoint bridge keys (email vs phone).
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: A card for a Gmail-heavy human (Ramon, Daljeet) gets BUILT from Gmail alone. WA-heavy buckets are BUILT from WA alone. Joining them requires either a) name+context inference by LLM (the 20% resolver layer) or b) richer Google Contacts exports with both phone+email per contact (currently zero).
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Expected. This is exactly where the LLM resolver earns its keep. NOT a deterministic-layer failure. But document: cross-channel join is an LLM step, not a deterministic step.

cracks_identified.name: google_contacts_export_missing_emails
cracks_identified.metric: Only 3 of 342 Google Contacts have an email address in the current export. 0 contacts have both a phone AND an email.
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: Eliminates the one mechanical bridge between WA (phone) and Gmail (email). Without it, every cross-channel merge depends on LLM inference.
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Verify: does gws fetch all Google Contacts fields, or is it stripping emails? Re-run gws with field-mask including emailAddresses. If the export really has only 3 emails in ground truth, move on — Sanchay's Google Contacts simply don't have emails.

cracks_identified.name: bulk_header_filter_weaker_than_localpart_rule
cracks_identified.metric: Bulk-header drop (list-unsubscribe + precedence:bulk) removes only 2.4% of gmail messages. Localpart+domain bot classifier removes 26.79% of distinct senders.
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: Bulk-header check is almost superfluous — the cyphersol/skydo/luma/accounts.dev noreply senders don't set List-Unsubscribe. The localpart rule is the real workhorse. Not a crack — just a note that the bulk-header filter is redundant and could be cheaper.
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Optional. Keep both for belt-and-suspenders; they're essentially free.

cracks_identified.name: no_dormant_then_recent_pattern_in_corpus
cracks_identified.metric: 0 DM chats match the dormant-then-reawakened seed pattern (Seed 5 from EDA).
cracks_identified.impact_on_pipeline: Can't test the 'going-cold' card flavor at this time. Either (a) Sanchay genuinely has no reawakened dormant relationships in the current corpus, or (b) the corpus is WA-only and doesn't yet include Gmail/Calendar which might show it. Defer: test going-cold once Gmail is included as a channel signal.
cracks_identified.fix_before_cli: Drop Seed 5 from the initial end-to-end test set. Re-add once we have multi-channel observations.

verdict_detail: Deterministic rule coverage is EXCELLENT where the rules apply (99.8% phone normalize, 99.85% LID bridge, 100% email canonicalize, 26.8% bot drop). Cross-source joining is shallow because Sanchay's Google Contacts export contains almost no emails — so there is no mechanical bridge between WhatsApp (phone-keyed) and Gmail (email-keyed). This is a DATA PROPERTY, not a rule failure. The 82% singleton rate reflects reality: most WA contacts are LID-only group lurkers with no name and no phone bridge. The deterministic layer correctly refuses to invent identities for them. What the CLI/resolver work must add: (1) seed filtering that drops unresolvable lurkers before any LLM call, (2) explicit LLM-step for WA<->Gmail cross-channel join, (3) enforced @lid-phone-column guard as a unit-tested rule.
